query: ChargePoint EV charging app review demo tutorial

transcripts.title: ChargePoint HOme Flex Review
transcripts.transcript:
hey everybody welcome back to the channel my intention with this channel is definitely not to become a EV charging Channel um although that's kind of been what a lot of videos have been about recently I also don't tend to do product reviews um unless there's just not any reviews already on YouTube and there are quite a few videos already on YouTube about this particular EV charger but the reason decided to make a review video on this charger is because uh one just as kind of a followup as the last

few videos have been kind of why you shouldn't buy a particular charger and all the problems that I encountered with uh with the last charger or two because this one has been just absolutely fantastic and also because most of the existing reviews really focus a lot more on installation and not really operation and I was most interested in seeing how the app worked on this um on this particular charger before I purchased it and there's very little uh very little examples about that um so today we're

going to do just kind of a quick review no installation uh there's plenty of videos on that to see and just what I like and uh why I think this may be one of the best value Chargers um on the market so hardware-wise there's not really a lot to talk about here it's not very exciting looking it's not very fancy looking um it's not ugly but it's not impressive looking but uh where it lacks and looks it really makes up for and functionality um so just a couple quick things um one

is this cable is great um there's already been um some some reviews and testing done on this cable so I won't get into that um stateof charge has a great a great video on this charger where they put this in a in a freezer and show how it's still flexible um so this this C is is great because it it has the flexibility of the Alum ulum charger I used to have um but it's not nearly as thick and heavy and clunky and then this handle is also great it's unlike any any charging handle I've ever

used before it's just got a great Contour to it a great feel in the hand the quality is great and then of course this little dock um it just automatically pivots up when you pull it out and nice click and just hangs hangs out like that so you know that's great that's been covered um so we won't spend a lot of time on that um I really like how the unit itself has this EAS easily removable cover so that comes off there's a couple um thumb screws or or screws that you can put a coin in and turn and to get to

all the electronics not going to do that now because again this isn't really an installation video but it's just it's it's a great design overall so if you saw my last video you know um uh I really like um the charger to tell me what it's doing um I like to know at a glance what's going on uh the ULM charger had some had some notification lights in the front but they were hard to remember what they were doing this this one is is super easy um so if it's green it's on it's ready to go it's not

doing anything but it's ready to go um so you can see that kind of green glow around the charge port um so let's uh let's see what happens when we plug it in so it's now blue and once the car starts communicating there it goes it's pulsing so when it's pulsing like this that means it is delivering a charge to the vehicle it's just that easy it's easy to spot across the room it's easy to know what's going on and um you know that's that's basically it and I'll show

you here in a minute but when the charges complete it'll just change to a solid blue light um so yes it is perfectly capable of telling you when the charge is complete um this one handles it a little differently than um the Altium charger did I believe the altum charger um pretty much as soon as that draw from the car went down to nothing it pretty much immediately went to a a status light of hey I'm done plugged in but not charging um this one works a little bit more like I would have liked to see the UniFi one work um

or or what I was suggesting is that you know after it's detected and no power being delivered um for a certain amount of time then it tells you the charge is complete I would say if I have a complaint against the charger um I would say I'd like to see that time frame tightened up a little bit um it seems that it can be anywhere from 20 to 30 minutes um between the time that the car actually notifies me it's done charging and the charger notifies me not a huge deal really um but it seems like they

could tighten that up a little bit um and basically the notification it gives you is more long lines of hey the car is pulling very little power um it's probably done charging or something along those lines and then interestingly enough because I know this was a topic of much discussion on the last video the app does have a a duration counter so like the charger before this one it does um it does track the entire time the cord is plugged in so if you plug this in at 5:00 p.m. and it finishes charging

at 700 but you don't plug unplug it until 5:00 a.m. the next morning then the stats on that particular charge will be 12 hours so that is the way that works on this charger I can see the argument both ways again you know if I'm going to if I'm going to have a little bit of a quibble I would say uh and this is nitpicking but we need to have a toggle in there that says hey um it let's have it work this way or let's have it work that way um let's have it work where when I detect the charge is probably

complete to stop the timer or no I want the timer to be the whole time it's plugged in and again a simple adjustment in the app for my other little complaint about the the duration time um maybe have the ability to to dial that in a little bit and maybe if it doesn't detect any High draw of power for 5 minutes um you know maybe maybe make that adjustable um but speaking of the app let's take a look at it because that's like the big thing that I wanted to see before I bought this I wanted a

smart charger I wanted an app I wanted data and it was hard for me to find a lot of that information on this charger okay so here we are in the charge Point app um the cool thing about this app is it uses the same app that you do for their DC fast charger so if you've already created an account and used their app to to charge at their DC fast Chargers along interstates then you've already got everything you need and you're already somewhat familiar with the way this app looks um so when you uh

when you get this charger um you'll need to uh add it to your account add it to the app um so it is tied to your account specifically in fact um a little tip for you um I have uh in passing noticed I have in passing read about how um if you don't remove it from your account and then someone else can't add it to theirs uh so this is presumably a theft deterrent um but just keep that in mind if you're going to pick up one of these secondhand from eBay or somewhere to make sure that that seller is going

to remove it from their account otherwise you're going to have a problem um so here's uh here's the main tab uh for the uh for your home charger of course you can see at the bottom where you have the other uh tabs to to go to the map to look for you know DC fast Chargers that kind of thing your account but we'll we'll focus you you've probably seen that if you've charged a DC fast Chargers we're going to focus on this home charger um so you can set a schedule I don't because my power is the

same price 247 and also the car has that ability built in so I don't really have a need for that and so we've got the remind me to plug in so I do have that set that's a nice feature to have um although my car has that ability through OnStar um and so I've already actually got that set up but the nice thing about this is the OnStar notifications tend to be inconsistent like sometimes they just stop working for a few days so this is a nice safety net um so here's where we set our charge

current limit I've got a 60 amp breaker so I've got it set at 48 amps and we've got the settings um so you can name your charger you've got um uh you can choose which utility you have which is really cool because then it will um pull data from that utility for your address from that provider and it will uh calculate your charging costs which I think is a great feature and it's one of the big big reasons I chose this charger in fact that is definitely something that UniFi should add to their charger um in a

future update so we can see the software version and everything on the charger um set the LED brightness reconfigure your Wi-Fi all that kind of good stuff there the remove from account that you would want to do if you were selling it all right so that's the boring part let's talk about the exciting part activity um so this is really cool um this uh shows you up at the top about how much money you've spent for the month so we've spent about $40 charging this car so far this month and then you

can see each charging session down here so if we click on any of these um we get a really cool graph um and it shows uh the approximate cost and the energy delivered um and again you can kind of see how it does keep counting you can see where I plugged it in when I got home that evening and then I unplugged it before I left for work um so that's why it's kind of flatlined for majority of it because it it only took a couple hours to charge back up and um you know and then it was just doing

nothing for the rest of the time so I do kind of wish you could you could dial that in but you know that that's my only uh my only complaint so we can also click these uh little buttons here to switch over to energy for the month and estimated distance now it uses that based on stats it knows about your car that you've previously entered on your account um so those are you know these are all estimates but it's really kind of cool to to be able to scroll through and kind of see some of these stats at a

glance um and then if we scroll back far enough you can see these these blue ones are our home charges and then uh we can look and see that these orange ones back over the summer where we're on vacation those are the DC fast Chargers so one little bug I've noticed in the app and it's deciding to do it right now is even though it shows it's charging I can't actually see the current charge sometimes it does this like usually you can actually click on one of these for the one you're actually in the middle of

and see see a graph so far and that kind of thing so I'm not sure why it but it'll eventually catch back up I'll try to check on it here in a minute maybe we can get some footage of it okay so now that the app has decided to show us the current charging session um so you know you can see there that's what it looks like when it's in progress got the little animation gives you some general stats about what has happened so far this doesn't happen often most of the time you know that does come up um but I will

say even when this you know the current activity feature is working as it should um you know it's not like super real time I'm guessing if this feature was just absolutely real time down to the second that would put a lot of strain on on whatever servers that charge point is using so this kind of tends to update you know every few minutes or so U depending on when you look at it but you can see how much um it's pulled since its last update which was about in this case about six minutes ago so it's not

like I said it's not absolute real time but it does give you a general idea of what's going on all right so you can see what the charger does um now that the battery is full charged um we've switched to a solid blue ring around the uh charging holster and then if we switch back over to the app here uh you can see it shows fully charged um and uh you can see the overview of of that charge says complete and we do have a notification here um I also got an email um which those are all options for

notifications um sadly this is one of the many times when OnStar does not send me a text to let me know the car has finished charging so I can't tell you the exact um delay between the charge Point realizing it and the car um but that's kind of what that looks like once it's completed charging and here's the uh email notification that you get kind of an overview of that charging session all righty that about does it for this quick video hope this helps somebody that's trying to decide what charger um

that they're going to get for their Eevee um I can't recommend this one enough I mean it's not perfect but it's really really good um for the for the now three different Chargers I've had experience with this this one is uh is definitely my favorite so far um I you know check out the one uh the video I just did on the UniFi station if you haven't seen that yet I think that one's really promising and maybe if the software ever matures on that that one could be um could be a great option of

course it's quite a bit more expensive um they do have a cheaper model available so you know there again if once their software mature that could be a contender for sure but until then um that's all for this video and I hope you'll consider subscribing and checking out some of the other uh content we have on this channel thanks for watching

transcripts.title: The chargepoint flex app isnt perfect
transcripts.transcript:
if you're looking to buy the charge Point home Flex charger you've probably watched a lot of review videos already that gush about the ability to schedule charging but you aren't getting the full picture I bought this level two charger specifically for the scheduled charging features and if I had known then what I know now I probably would have just saved my money so in this video we're going to talk about the charge Point H Flex charger and what you really need to know before you commit to buying this

charger we have a Rav 4 Prime that has a terrible scheduling system for charging while most electric cars have a scheduling system that allows you to set charge times to match your time of use rates from the electric company Toyota decided to go their own way and the solution they came up with is not that great for more detail on that I'll talk about it in a different video so I decided to get a reliable charger with scheduling functions from a reputable name in EV charging instead of wasting

time on cheap Amazon brands that might set my house or my car on fire charge point is one of the biggest names in EV charging so I assumed this would be a good solid choice and although I couldn't tell exactly how scheduling worked from all the videos that I watched before buying it I figured it had to be pretty decent since this is charge point we're talking  about now first I want to say it is really easy to set set a schedule in the charge Point app and import a schedule based on your local utility I'm in

California on pg& so it was super simple to set the charging window but if you're hoping to set up more than one charging window you are out of luck there is just one window that you can set to charge in that basic functionality is also available on board my KERO plug-in hybrid from 2022 so I thought well this isn't as full featured as I expected but at least I have the basic functionality that the Rav 4 Prime lacks so with the charge Point home Flex installed I believe I can now just plug in the Rav 4

Prime at any time and it will now only charge during non peak hours that's what I thought would happen anyway but unfortunately I was  wrong in actual usage the charge Point home Flex charger will randomly disregard the schedule it's not a user error issue it's not because I'm plugging the car in unplugging and plugging back in rapidly to set a manual charge it's just that the home Flex likes to disregard its schedule randomly if you do a little internet research you will discover that other people have

this problem too it is impossible to predict when the charge Point H Flex charger will just ignore the set scheduling window but it happens semi-regularly one evening this happened when my wife plugged in the car and the charge Point immediately started charging in the middle of P peak hours I didn't realize it had happened until the next day when I was looking at the activity logs and realized that it had pulled a full charge during peak  hours in California if you pull power at peak hours you're paying a lot for

electricity the cost to fuel your fancy electrified car per mile starts to become equivalent or even more than the cost of fueling comparable gas car this completely negates one of the Main Financial incentives for owning an electric car to realize any fuel savings with your electric car versus a gas powerered car you need to charge during non peak hours if you charge at peak hours you'll save no money compared to driving a gas car that is why you get a charger that allows you to set a schedule but unfortunately you cannot

rely on the charge Point home Flex to manage that schedule reliably so if that's the main reason you're looking at the charge Point hlex charger you might want to look  elsewhere reading through the internet there are two solutions to this problem first you can reboot the charger within the app on your phone you go to this screen and then click reboot and then according to reports it'll work correctly for a while until it doesn't then you repeat this process for the rest of your life or until charge Point

fixed is this bizarre software bug another solution The Hive mine recommends is that you should just rely on your car's internal scheduling if you can but that may also not be foolproof as I talked about in a previous video for some unknown reason some EVS don't have reliable scheduling systems you'll set a charging schedule and it will work most of the time but one morning you might wake up and discover that your car just didn't pull a charge and now you can't do what you thought you're going

to do I've seen reports of these kinds of hiccups from literally every brand of EV but it's totally unpredictable and it doesn't even consistently happen from vehicle to vehicle some people have absolutely zero issue with charging reliability and some people end up seeing weird hiccups fairly frequently I used to have a KERO EV and it was a decent car but the scheduling proved really unreliable so to fix that I used the delay function on a simpler cheaper level two charger that I had that was a

clunky somewhat inconvenient solution but it was 100% reliable I don't have that EV anymore for reasons and if you want to hear those reasons just drop me a comment down below other than those two solutions the fix for the anguish and irritation this might cause you is to just accept that software sometimes messes up and mistakes are going to happen unless you can find a truly reliable level two charger the real practical solution besides just changing your mindset about it is to have your app ready your phone ready and when you

plug in you just pop it in open up your app and check to make sure that it doesn't just randomly start charging when you don't want it to the issue really is that it'll just start charging when it's not supposed to right when you plug in I don't think it's going to just randomly start charging a couple hours later off of the schedule if that does happen and it's happened to you drop me a comment down below but that would seem totally bizarre so again once you've plugged it in you check to make sure if

it's on schedule it should be not charging if it starts you'll see it and you can just hit stop and then make sure it goes when it's supposed to on its normal schedule I don't want to make this sound all bad though there are two things I really like about using the charge Point app one is that I can just start and stop charging from my phone whenever I want so I don't have to walk down to the car to do anything the second thing I really like is that I can see a log of all the charging activity

that's been happening and I can see how much charging happened and all that that isn't that necessary but it's kind of cool to have that information at my fingertips it helps me understand when we're charging and how that's related to our solar system that we just got of course all of this relies on a stable Wi-Fi connection for the charge Point charger so hopefully my Wi-Fi doesn't give out randomly after the warranty period as I've read seems to happen occasionally to some owners of the home

Flex now here's where I have to turn to you for help because I don't have deep expertise or experience with level two Chargers I have only owned two in my life so I know people out there might have more experience with different Chargers so if you have information that could be helpful for me and everybody else out there please drop your comment down below and let us know what Chargers are actually reliable 100% of the time with their scheduling functions for me the most reliable setup has been this

primecom charger with built-in delay functions it's not a well-known brand and it's relatively inexpensive and all I can say is that it has worked reliably for me for the last 2 years it always charges when I plug it in and when I use the built-in delay function it always charges it has never failed when I was using its built-in delay function the interface however is not my favorite because the buttons are basically invisible and there's no physical feedback when you're pressing the

buttons you only see a change on the very tiny screen okay so looking at the prime com charger there is no button here it's just like totally flush you only figure this out from the instructional manual so you press on this little plug thing and it kind of eventually there so you can see the delay went to four I pressed it again it went to six this is a two-hand job I cannot hold the camera and do this at the same time because you have to like press in the spot really hard and just the right way there

there see now it changed to 4 hours 6 hours so this is and no zero okay so you have to keep pressing this non-existent button just this area here until it registers and then it tells you so you have two four or 6 hour delays available so if you wanted to delay at like 8 10 9 whatever 12 you don't have that choice so if you're like in a pg& area in California and you want to delay your charging until after midnight and you're done with your car for 6 p.m. you're just going to need to wait and like

figure out how to push this really frustrating little thing all I got it to 4 hours and eventually you get it didn't used to be this hard but it's gotten much harder anyway clearly I'm not in love with this that said I may go back to using this charger instead of the charge Point home Flex because it has a longer cable the charge Point home Flex cable is only 23 ft long the prime com cable that I have is 30 ft long and those 7 ft make a big difference 23 ft I got to get it over there okay now

this is 30 ft of cable with the prime conom charger and having more cable definitely creates a little bit more of a tangled mess but as you can see I can walk all the way over here and I can reach this with absolutely no problem at all with the 30ft charging cable I don't have to back the car into the driveway in order to get the plug to reach I can just go nose in and I can get the cable all the way around the car with no issue for now though I'm going to keep using the charge Point H Flex

charger and just make sure that it doesn't start charging randomly when we plug in if you know of a really good reliable charger with great scheduling functions that don't fail please drop a comment down below it'll be a huge help to me and everybody else out there and if you like this video please subscribe and remember to keep it simple

transcripts.title: ChargePoint home flex feature that is great but can cost you a lot more
transcripts.transcript:
okay I'm going to share something that you really need to know if you have a home Flex uh charger and you have
EVs and your uh Power company's got time of day rates even about the time day rates you really need to know this CU I
didn't know it this is our home Flex charger that I installed myself back a little over a year ago right after we
got this 2023 bolt EV it's worked real well no complaints about it no problems
at all charges up to 48 amps pretty close to what the maximum this car can
handle and uh so um this Chevy bolt EV can handle up to about 11.5 Kow
and we got this model 3 from my son back in March of this year and uh and we we use this level two charger to charge
both of them routinely we're always charging but this is great because with the solar panels we got down there in the pasture and so forth we save a lot of
money on gas and hassle we go somewhere we just come home plug it in we're good to go but what's happened is our power
company has gone to time of day rates they just recently did that right now for the winter period
since November 1st the time of day rate for here we in Blue Ridge Electric Co-op peak time is 6:00 to 9:00 and the way it
works is Blue Ridge has um changed their normal rate per kilowatt hour from like
15.25 I believe cents per kilowatt hour down to 13.9 cents which okay okay that's good they dropped the rate
however they added Peak periods it's different for the winter and summer for the winter now it's between 6 and 9:00
a.m. in the morning and what they do is they charge you an extra $13 per
kilowatt for uh per kilowatt for every kilowatt you use during that Peak
period as you can see if you're charging at something like 11 and a half kilowatts that can get real expensive
all it takes is charging one of these things at the wrong time you got a big problem well to prevent that
risk I found out I could program this charge point for a schedule it so it
doesn't charge during those time periods and it's worked really well this is the charge Point
app from the home screen which I've tapped on home down here go to
schedule you can schedule it for weekdays or for weekends I've got it scheduled to start charging at 9:30 a.m.
and end at 5:30 a.m. in the morning you can't see it uh let me get
rid of that there it is fine same thing there what that means is between 5:30 in the morning and 9:30 it won't ch charge
and this has worked really well our actual peak times from our power company is 6 to 9 I just gave myself an extra
half hour on either side to be to be safe but that's how you do it and this does work well but this morning I had gone out
actually gone out to vote and came back and uh I had took the Tesla this car was sitting here it would finished charging
last night so it was no problem this morning and and uh the uh uh Tesla I
took it off the vot and I came back and I said okay let me go ahead and charge it up so I moved the charge cord here
from The Vault real quickly I moved it over to here because no big deal put the adapter on it plug it into the Tesla
well let me just check and make sure the charge point system was working right and I checked on the
screen and I'm not going to show you that now you don't need enough but it was charging and it was only 9:15 I had set the time of day rates from 5:30 a.m.
to 9:30 I thought what's going on it's not supposed to be charging it wasn't hurting us CU it was
after the 9:00 period got charge Point customer service on the phone and he told me exactly what had
happened they have a feature built into these charge Point
Chargers so so that in case you don't have your phone with you and you need to charge you can do what they call a
double bump on the cord which is If instead of just plugging it
in you you you uh take it out for 5 seconds plug it back in it disables the scheduling
function what had happen is I'd taken the cord out of this car plugged it into the Tesla fast enough that it thought it
was a double bump apparently that's apparently what happened and because he had the customer
service agent at charge Point had a record of that double bump and uh so he said to fix that he
said to go back to the schedule I just can pull the Char the cord out of the car wait for 10 seconds plug it back in
and it's back on the schedule I did not know any of that but not not knowing that could get expensive that's why I
wanted to share this video with people so so I hope you found this useful I
don't know what other types of chargers might do a similar thing as this charge point it's good charger we like it all this is work we really love having these
EVs and avoiding gas stations and all that but you need to know this especially if you're on time of day rais
with your uh Power Company so if you got something out of this please like the video video uh check out our other links
subscribe we'd really appreciate that thanks a lot

transcripts.transcript:
all right today I'm reviewing our charge Point home Flex level two EV charger 240 volts about a 23 is foot long cable I've
got it adapted by a oneac uh adapter cable which is solely because the wall outlet did not match the charger that I

Point home charger uh overall nothing really negative to say about this uh UL certified the company's been around

and uh have it do whatever you want you know typically your car can do that too the ev9 of course can do that but uh it's obviously uh also capable to just

overlapped with with uh out in public so it's nice to just have your home charger do what you tell it to do um yeah other
than that um the best thing I think about this particular charger to me was that I got on sale and that because it's old uh by
old I mean the company has been around a long time and it's been a long offered model uh the rebates that I got locally through the uh electric company were
sufficient to basically make it a a no-brainer like even if I wasn't getting an electric vehicle at some point I probably would have bought it anyway
solely because uh for 100 bucks it's worth you know the resale on the house or whatever like it's a nice thing to have but I knew we were going to be
getting either a plug-in hybrid or an electric vehicle you know within the year it's just a matter of of time unfortunately with Kia they're uh a
little behind on things uh besides that I would say the biggest things that it's not super super flashy I mean yes it is
pulsing blue which is pretty cool uh but it's not like a you know most aesthetically peeling thing I've seen
some units from autel Wolf block and I think Grizzly that I thought looked cooler than this one and you know maybe had
some extra advertised features but they are not offered for rebates on the electric Pro uh company so realistically

needed this or something like it to work in my case I'm actually really happy this looks really sketchy um but with
the 45° twist in this cable being able to put it that way it allowed some ability to have it over further which I

to have had it either higher or off to the other side to be able to come down and plug into that outet properly and

I'm happy with this orientation now beyond that I I do recommend this product I would be tempted to look at other options if you either don't care

transcripts.transcript:

its own RFID Card uh and they don't talk
to each other so it's different than

for example behind me here is the shell

activate this with my charge Point
account and
similarly again where that blue id4 is

activate the charge Point session so
